options_considered.name: A - Blanket AI automation tax or broad slowdown
B - Let labor markets adjust with minimal intervention
D - Narrow temporary tax in defined sectors

recommendation_rule: highest-strength option wins; ties broken by option order in options[]; strength order strong > conditional > moderate > weak > unsupported

affects_options: D - Narrow temporary tax in defined sectors
rankings.rule_trace: C-025 is the sole claim the argument map links to the recommended option's node (05_argument_map.mmd: Q --> D[Targeted compact C-025]; that node maps to options[] 'C - Targeted transition compact', strength strong). Flipping C-025 supported->unsupported removes that node's only supporting claim, dropping the recommended option one tier from strong to moderate. Under recommendation_rule (strong > conditional > moderate > weak) 'D - Narrow temporary tax' (conditional) then outranks it, so the winning option changes from C to D.

C-001 sits under the 'automation can harm exposed workers' branch (05_argument_map.mmd node A, which also carries C-020). Flipping C-001 leaves that branch still supported by C-020, and node A is not the recommended option, so neither the winner (C) nor its strength tier changes.

C-006 sits under the 'special AI tax weakly supported' branch (05_argument_map.mmd node C, which also carries C-026). Flipping it leaves C-026 still supporting that branch, and the branch is not the recommended option, so the winner (C) and its tier are unchanged.

X-001 (Broad AI tax versus targeted tax reform) attaches to the weakly-supported tax branch (05_argument_map.mmd node C), not to the recommended option's node. Reversing its resolution_status changes neither which option wins nor the recommended option's strength tier.
